summary: Excellent 90s show featuring Bruce Hornsby's debut and the unique final “Gimme Some Lovin’” performance, recording quality appears acceptable but unconfirmed.
review: Reviewers rave about this early‑1990s concert as a historic moment – it marks Bruce Hornsby’s first night with the Dead alongside Vince Welnick, and includes the last‑ever rendition of “Gimme Some Lovin’,” the only one without Brent Mydland. No specific recording flaws are noted, suggesting the audio is at least listenable, though the source type isn’t identified.

ai_rating.rationale: A single 5‑star review highlights a historic and musically significant show; recording quality is not discussed, so rating leans heavily on performance merit.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No explicit comments about sound clarity or mix; presumed adequate for listening.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Historical first‑time pairing of Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick energizes the set, capped by a unique closing “Gimme Some Lovin’” that fans note as a one‑off.

band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce Hornsby's first appearance with the band is highlighted.
